archimate:ApplicationInterface.name: Geo-Localization
archimate:ApplicationInterface.documentation:
Definition: The Geo-localization Application Service is a web service that provides functionality for determining and retrieving the geographic location of a device, user, or point of interest using various data sources and techniques. It enables applications to integrate location-based features and services, such as mapping, routing, proximity search, and geofencing, into their functionality. The Geo-localization Application Service utilizes a combination of technologies, including GPS, IP geolocation, Wi-Fi positioning, cellular network data, and geospatial algorithms, to accurately determine and track the location information. It offers a standardized and accessible interface for developers to query and utilize location data in their applications, allowing for enhanced user experiences, targeted services, and location-aware functionality
Source: European Location Framework (ELF)
Source reference: https://www.elfproject.eu/
LOST view: TVA-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/Geo-LocalizationApplicationInterface
ABB name: egovera:Geo-LocalizationApplicationInterface
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/Geo-LocalizationApplicationInterface
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/WebServiceApplicationInterface

properties.value: The Geo-localization Application Service is a web service that provides functionality for determining and retrieving the geographic location of a device, user, or point of interest using various data sources and techniques. It enables applications to integrate location-based features and services, such as mapping, routing, proximity search, and geofencing, into their functionality. The Geo-localization Application Service utilizes a combination of technologies, including GPS, IP geolocation, Wi-Fi positioning, cellular network data, and geospatial algorithms, to accurately determine and track the location information. It offers a standardized and accessible interface for developers to query and utilize location data in their applications, allowing for enhanced user experiences, targeted services, and location-aware functionality
